evals.prompt: Build a settings page with a form that has text fields for name and email, a select dropdown for language preference, some checkboxes for notification options, and a submit button. Use Fortal theming with indigo accent. Make it look polished.
evals.expected_output: A complete Flutter widget wrapped in FortalScope(accentColor: .indigo) using the single enum-based Fortal constructors, including FortalSelect(triggerVariant: .surface, contentVariant: .solid) without redundant per-entry preset styling, plus FortalTextField, FortalCheckbox, and FortalButton preset widgets.

evals.prompt: Create a custom-styled button that has a gradient background, rounded corners, scales down on press, and glows on hover. Don't use Fortal — build a fully custom style from scratch using the Remix fluent API. Show me the complete code.
evals.expected_output: A RemixButton with a fully custom RemixButtonStyler using the fluent API — gradient, borderRounded, .onPressed with scale, .onHovered with shadow/glow, .animate(AnimationConfig...) for transitions. Uses RemixButtonStyler (not the removed RemixButtonStyle) throughout.

evals.prompt: I'm using FortalIconButton but I need a long-press action and a semantic label on it. How do I do that?
evals.expected_output: Explains that FortalIconButton forwards the complete RemixIconButton behavior surface and shows the single enum-based constructor with variant, onLongPress, and semanticLabel supplied directly.

evals.prompt: I have a RemixButton with one icon and need the icon after the label without changing the widget arguments. Can styling do that? What happens if the button later gets both a leading and trailing icon?
evals.expected_output: Uses RemixButtonStyler().iconAlignment(IconAlignment.end) and explains that the style controls placement whenever exactly one icon exists, regardless of which icon argument supplied it. Explains that with both icons Remix preserves leading -> label -> trailing order instead of allowing the alignment override to collapse or reorder the pair.

evals.prompt: Can I call showRemixDialog from a subtree that does not have MixScope? I only need a plain Remix dialog, not Fortal tokens.
evals.expected_output: Explains that showRemixDialog works without MixScope and only clones tokens plus modifier order when a current scope exists. Distinguishes the scope-optional route helper from Fortal-styled content, which still needs FortalScope so FortalTokens can resolve.

evals.prompt: I pass a small Column with ordinary Text widgets as RemixTooltip.tooltipChild. Can the tooltip's custom label styler apply to those nested Text widgets?
evals.expected_output: Explains that RemixTooltip applies its resolved RemixTooltipStyler.label(...) spec through DefaultTextStyle, so ordinary descendant Text widgets inherit the custom label style while retaining the arbitrary tooltipChild structure.
